LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JULY-30-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP LLC AND JACKSON CHANG'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (FAC) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF OBJECTIONS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM WAIVER IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT ARENA NIGHTLIGHT GROUP LLC'S FAILURE TO TIMELY SERVE DISCOVERY RESPONSES WAS THE RESULT OF INADVERTENCE AND THAT ARENA HAS SUBSEQUENTLY SERVED RESPONSES TO THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY THAT IS IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH ITS DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: demurrer to all three causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and off calendar as to the second and third causes of action for unfair competition and financial elder abuse. Plaintiff Richard Einselen adequately alleges a claim that he was damaged by ANG's failure to pay the loan it assumed in the stock purchase agreement and that the failure to pay was a breach of the stock purchase agreement. Einselen dismissed the second and third causes of action. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ARENA NIGHTLIFE GROUP, LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Arena Nightlife Group LLC and Jackson Chang's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend.

The FAC alleges that defendants breached the Stock Purchase Agreement (Agreement) attached as Exhibit 1 to the FAC by failing to make payments on a loan that was assumed by Arena Nightlife in the Agreement. Under the Agreement, Arena Nightlife bought all shares of Qualia, Inc., plaintiff Richard Einselen's former company. It is not clear from the FAC whether Qualia alone was the borrower of the loan or whether Einselen was obliged under the loan agreement as a co-borrower or guarantor. If he personally had no obligations under the loan agreement, it is not clear why he made a payment on the loan after Arena Nightlife defaulted and how he was harmed by the default. Einselen has leave to amend to clarify his relationship to the loan.

Defendants also demur to the second and third causes of action, for violations of the Unfair Competition Law and elder abuse statutes. The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend. A breach of contract alone does not satisfy either of these statutes. In addition, Einselen does not plead his age as of the time of the conduct he complains of.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
